bindings.quest_action: retrace their steps and find the source of the affliction
bindings.quest_focus: a strange substance found on the victim's skin
bindings.quest_stakes: the affliction spreading to others in the settlement
bindings.quest_threat: an unknown force in the wilderness that shatters minds
bindings.quest_origin: a walk into the woods that came back wrong

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, something is wrong with {target_npc}. Deeply, unsettlingly wrong.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Three days ago they were perfectly normal. Then they wandered into the woods {location_hint} and came back... different. Speaking in fragments, drawing strange symbols on the walls, refusing to eat. The alchemist examined them and found traces of {quest_item} on their skin: {quest_focus} that shouldn't exist naturally in this region. Whatever they encountered out there changed them. I need someone to {quest_action} before {quest_stakes}.
dialogueChunks.outro: I've seen illness and I've seen grief. This is neither. {quest_threat} broke their mind, and I need to know what.

playerResponses.text: I'll follow their trail and find the source. Keep them safe until I return.
Strange substances and broken minds? This feels dangerous. I'll look into it carefully.
Cursed territory and madness? I'm not touching this.

bindings.quest_action: retrieve a sample of the contamination from its source
bindings.quest_focus: a contaminated alchemical batch from an unknown cave
bindings.quest_stakes: the settlement losing more people to the whispers
bindings.quest_threat: an ancient corruption seeping through tainted ingredients
bindings.quest_origin: a well-meaning tonic brewed from unknowingly contaminated reagents

dialogueChunks.intro: Careful where you step, {player_name}. See those markings on the ground? {target_npc} has been carving them all night.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: It started after the alchemist brewed a new tonic using {quest_item} from an unknown source. Three people drank it: two recovered after a few hours of confusion. {target_npc} didn't. They've been speaking to things that aren't there, claiming something in the water at {location} is alive and whispering to them. The alchemist is terrified: says the {quest_item} they used was contaminated with {quest_threat}. The original batch came from a cave {location_hint} where the {enemy_type} have been unusually active.
dialogueChunks.outro: We need a sample of whatever contaminated that batch. Without it, the alchemist can't brew an antidote and {target_npc} may never come back to us.

playerResponses.text: Contaminated ingredients and whispers in the water? I'll find the source.
If the alchemist caused this, the alchemist should fix it. But I'll get the sample.
Ancient contamination and cave creatures? I value my sanity too much.

bindings.quest_action: investigate the crumbling tower from the shared nightmares
bindings.quest_focus: a recurring nightmare plaguing the entire settlement
bindings.quest_stakes: the settlement descending into collective insanity
bindings.quest_threat: something ancient in a crumbling tower broadcasting into people's dreams
bindings.quest_origin: shared visions of a tower and a demanding voice

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I need your help. The settlement is frightened and I don't blame them.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: People in {location} have been having the same nightmare for a week: vivid images of a crumbling tower {location_hint} and a voice demanding {quest_item}. Most shake it off by morning, but {target_npc} hasn't slept in three days. They wander the streets at dawn, eyes glazed, muttering directions to a place they've never been. The alchemist brewed a sleeping draft but {target_npc} fought it off like they were possessed. Whatever is causing {quest_focus}, it's centered on that tower.
dialogueChunks.outro: I don't believe in curses, {player_name}. But I believe in what I'm seeing. And {quest_threat} terrifies me.

playerResponses.text: Shared nightmares and a crumbling tower? I'll investigate. Stay close to {target_npc}.
This sounds like more than a bad dream. I'll go, but I need to know everything you know.
Cursed towers and possessions? Find a priest, not an adventurer.

bindings.quest_action: retrieve a fresh sample from the quarry for the alchemist to study
bindings.quest_focus: a mind-warping artifact pulled from an old quarry
bindings.quest_stakes: the victim losing their humanity entirely
bindings.quest_threat: a buried artifact that drives anyone near it to violent paranoia
bindings.quest_origin: an innocent discovery in abandoned tunnels that turned lethal

dialogueChunks.intro: Don't be alarmed by the barricaded house, {player_name}. That's... that's where we're keeping {target_npc}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: They used to be the kindest soul in {location}. Then they found something in the old quarry {location_hint}: {quest_focus} covered in {quest_item} residue. Since they brought it back, they've been violent, paranoid, attacking anyone who comes close. The alchemist says the residue has properties unlike anything in their books. They need a fresh sample from the quarry to study, but the {enemy_type} have moved into the tunnels since {target_npc} disturbed whatever was down there.
dialogueChunks.outro: We've locked them in for their own safety. But every hour their eyes look a little less like a person's. Please hurry.

playerResponses.text: An artifact that drives people mad? I'll get the sample and bring back answers.
If there's something dangerous in that quarry, someone should have sealed it long ago. I'll go.
Whatever turned them violent could do the same to me. I'll pass.
